exemplar.title: To what extent is the idealization of motherhood deconstructed in the books Beloved by Toni Morrison and Breasts and Eggs by Mieko Kawakami, based on its contradictions and unattainable stereotypical image?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent is the idealization of motherhood deconstructed in the books Beloved by Toni Morrison and Breasts and Eggs by Mieko Kawakami, based on its contradictions and unattainable stereotypical image?

subject.title: English A Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **literary texts from diverse cultures, genres, and time periods**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis, interpretation, and understanding of how literature reflects and shapes human experience through themes, cultural contexts, and global issues**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Unseen literary text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay on studied works | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 40% |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation on global issues | 10 minutes | 10 minutes | 25% |

strengths: The research question is clearly stated, sharply focused and effectively frames the entire investigation.
Sections are explicitly sign-posted (context, single-text analysis, comparison, conclusion) and consistently tied back to the central inquiry.
The introduction and conclusion clearly communicate the purpose and demonstrate how each part addresses the research question.
weaknesses: The methodology is implicitâthere is no upfront outline of how close reading, socio-historical research and theoretical frameworks are balanced.
Some sources are referenced only once and the criteria for source selection are not fully articulated.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student communicates the topic and research question with precision and maintains a clear, purposeful structure throughout (evidenced by two band-5 strand assessments for topic clarity and question focus). However, the lack of an explicit methodology section (band-4) limits the completeness and transparency of methods. An aggregated band score of 5/6 accurately reflects strong focus and method with room to more deliberately articulate methodological choices.

strengths: Demonstrates secure and accurate knowledge of both novels, using detailed plot points and character motivations to support arguments.
Effectively integrates secondary scholarship (e.g., Shinohara 2017, Wike) to deepen contextual understanding.
Accurate and mostly consistent use of key literary and theoretical terms (patriarchy, bodily autonomy, stream of consciousness).
weaknesses: Occasional generalisations (e.g., âhistorically, women have not had much freedomâ) are not fully substantiated with nuanced evidence.
Minor slips in terminology precision and occasional lack of context for secondary sources (e.g., Munawar & Chaudhary 2022).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows clear and coherent understanding of primary texts and applies secondary material effectively, meriting the upper half of the 3â4 band. Occasional overgeneralisation and slight inconsistencies in terminology prevent a higher mark, so 4/6 suitably reflects sound knowledge and mostly consistent use of concepts.

strengths: A diverse bibliography of peer-reviewed articles, interviews and contextual studies underpins the investigation with appropriate relevance.
Close readings of key scenes (Setheâs declarations, Natsukoâs dialogues) are cogent and generally well supported.
The comparative synthesis effectively integrates themes across both novels, showing good critical engagement and connecting findings back to the research question.
weaknesses: Some broader claims are asserted rather than fully demonstrated and lack specific historical evidence.
Transitionsâparticularly between Japanese context and U.S. slavery legacyâare at times abrupt, weakening coherence.
Engagement with counterarguments and deeper interrogation of secondary critics is limited, reducing the depth of evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research selection and analysis are consistently relevant and demonstrate good analytical depth with supported conclusions, placing the work in the mid-to-upper range of the 7â9 band. However, gaps in substantiation, abrupt transitions and limited critical interrogation prevent it reaching the highest band. A score of 8/12 balances these strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: All required structural components (title page, contents page, section headings, conclusion, bibliography) are present and logically ordered.
Pagination, consistent line spacing, clear headings and a stated word count enhance readability and navigation.
weaknesses: Citation style alternates between hyperlinks and standard references, and the bibliography is not alphabetised.
Inconsistent formatting (leader dots, dash lengths, italics usage) and long URLs disrupt the visual coherence of the document.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets core structural and layout requirements, guiding the reader effectively (band 4 for structure). Nonetheless, inconsistencies in referencing style, ordering and minor formatting errors limit the work to the mid-band. A score of 3/4 appropriately reflects strong overall presentation with noticeable lapses.

strandSelections.reasoning: A solid spread of primary and secondary sources (eleven items spanning academic articles, interviews and a dictionary definition) is employed and integrated with parenthetical page references.  However, methodological explanation is implicit rather than explicit; the introduction does not outline how socio-historical research, literary theory or close reading will be balanced.  Source selection is generally apt, yet some remain unchallenged or are referenced only once, indicating room for a more deliberately crafted methodology.

Pagination, consistent line spacing and clear headings enhance readability, and the word count is stated.  Nonetheless, the bibliography is not alphabetised, citation style fluctuates between hyperlinks and standard references, and there are uneven dash lengths and italics usage.  These lapses show that while layout conventions are largely met, they are not flawlessly or uniformly applied.

The bibliography reflects a good range of relevant sourcesâincluding peer-reviewed articles, a contemporary interview and contextual studiesâmost of which are employed in the body of the essay to illuminate textual moments.  Although some references (e.g., the dictionary definition) add limited depth, overall the research base is appropriate and generally well-chosen for the question.

The student states a precise research topic in the title and reiterates it in the opening paragraphs.  Throughout the essay every major section (context, single-text analysis, comparison and conclusion) is clearly sign-posted and explicitly related back to the overall exploration of how the âidealisation of motherhood is deconstructed.â  Explanations of why the two novels have been selected and how the concept of motherhood will be defined show purposeful focus, so the reader never loses sight of the central line of inquiry.

The discussion is coherent, progressing from societal context to single-text analysis and finally to a comparative synthesis that addresses the research question.  Evaluation of secondary critics is present but tends to affirm rather than interrogate their arguments, and some counter-perspectives are missing.  Even so, the student maintains a generally critical stance and draws conclusions that are mostly substantiated by evidence.

Close readings of Setheâs declarations (âI am Belovedâ¦â) and Natsukoâs dialogues with Yuriko are cogently unpacked, and the essay moves logically from context to textual evidence to interpretation.  Nevertheless, some broader claimsâsuch as equating Japanese patriarchal constraints directly with slaveryâare asserted rather than fully demonstrated, so a portion of the analysis rests on inference more than proof.

All mandatory structural componentsâtitle page, contents page, clearly headed sections, conclusion and bibliographyâare present and logically ordered.  The structure guides the reader effectively from introduction through comparison to conclusion, fully supporting the essayâs purpose.

The student demonstrates secure knowledge of both novelsâplot points, character motivations and textual details are accurately recalledâand shows familiarity with socio-historical scholarship on Japanese single mothers and slavery-era motherhood.  Application of this material is usually effective, though a few secondary claims are taken at face value and the discussion occasionally relies on generalities (e.g., âhistorically, women have not had much freedom over their own bodiesâ) instead of nuanced evidence.

Key literary and theoretical termsâpatriarchy, bodily autonomy, free indirect discourse, stream of consciousnessâare used mostly correctly and help to advance the argument.  Minor slips (âantialâ for âvitalâ, occasional vague phrases such as âin a context where that is not possibleâ) show that terminology is not always precise, preventing full consistency.

The research question is sharply worded, narrow enough to be manageable and explicitly drives the structure of the discussion.  Each analytical section closes by linking its findings to âthe extentâ of deconstruction, demonstrating that the question governs the investigation rather than being an after-thought.

annotations.comment: Citation style alternates between hyperlink format and plain references; unify the referencing style for consistent presentation.
An extensive bibliography with diverse academic and primary sources demonstrates thorough research and supports the essayâs credibility.
The Morrison section uses primary text evidence to demonstrate how trauma shapes Setheâs experience of motherhood, reflecting secure knowledge of the novel.
The research question is clearly stated and framed in precise language, providing a strong focus that guides the subsequent analysis effectively.
The inclusion of a precise word count (3860) at the beginning demonstrates adherence to formatting requirements and assists in verifying compliance with the 4000-word limit.
The heading âExtended Essayâ is generic; a descriptive investigation title should be provided to clarify the specific focus of the essay immediately.
The transition from Japanese societal context to the legacy of U.S. slavery is abrupt; a linking sentence would improve coherence and guide the comparative analysis.
The table of contents is comprehensive and accurately maps section titles to page numbers, facilitating navigation and demonstrating clear structural organisation.
The leader dots and spacing in the contents entries are inconsistently applied, which slightly detracts from the visual clarity of the layout.
The introduction effectively contextualises both novels and clearly links their settings to the research question, establishing a coherent purpose and focus.

The essay lacks an explicit outline of the methodological approach (e.g., balance of close reading, socio-historical research, theoretical frameworks); an upfront methodology section would strengthen focus.
The section on Japanese single mothers is supported by recent scholarship (Shinohara, 2017), demonstrating effective use of secondary sources and domain knowledge.
The general statement âhistorically, women have not had much freedomâ is unsupported; incorporate specific historical examples or citations to substantiate this claim.
The analysis of Breasts and Eggs prioritises key scenes where societal judgment is articulated, showing careful selection of relevant textual evidence.
Following the Kawakami quote, the student should explicitly connect its meaning back to the research question to reinforce the analytical focus.
Integrating Kawakamiâs interview comments adds valuable secondary insight, deepening the contextual understanding of motherhood expectations.
This paragraph would benefit from a clearer explanation of how the interview quote specifically deconstructs the idealized image of motherhood.
The discussion of Natsukoâs acceptance of risk offers a nuanced interpretation that links her personal choices to broader themes of autonomy and liberation.
When citing Munawar & Chaudhary (2022), include brief context on their research method or theoretical stance to clarify the sourceâs authority.
Quotation punctuation (âSlave life; freed life- every dayâ¦â) is inconsistent; ensure accuracy and consistency against the original text.
The analysis of Setheâs infanticide would be enriched by engaging with counterarguments or critical perspectives on her decision.
Linking Sofia Wikeâs scholarship to Setheâs marginalization under slavery underscores effective synthesis of secondary research and textual analysis.
Long URLs in bibliography entries disrupt the layout; consider truncating or using footnotes to maintain consistent formatting.
The comparative section effectively synthesizes themes of single motherhood across both novels, showcasing integrative critical thinking.
The introduction of the Kawakami quote would benefit from a clear topic sentence that situates the example within the comparative argument.
The conclusion could be enhanced by reflecting on methodological limitations and suggesting avenues for further research to demonstrate reflexivity.
The bibliography entries are not in alphabetical order, making it harder to locate sources quickly; reorder them alphabetically by author surname.
The discussion of bodily autonomy and identity parallels is persuasive, showing accurate and consistent use of key concepts to advance the argument.
The analysis would be strengthened by contrasting divergent community responses in each novel to highlight thematic nuances, not just parallels.
The conclusion succinctly reiterates the research findings and clearly demonstrates how the essaysâ sections have addressed the central question.
